chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

parts.text: أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقِيلَ لَهُمَا إِنَّا نَشْتَرِي ثِيَاباً يُصِيبُهَا اَلْخَمْرُ
وَدَكُ اَلْخِنْزِيرِ عِنْدَ حَاكَتِهَا أَ نُصَلِّي

text: هَا قَبْلَ أَنْ نَغْسِلَهَا فَقَالاَ «نَعَمْ لاَ بَأْسَ إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ اَللَّهُ أَكْلَهُ وَ شُرْبَهُ وَ لَمْ يُحَرِّمْ لُبْسَهُ وَ مَسَّهُ وَ اَلصَّلاَةَ فِيهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.751 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) and Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) were asked:
"We buy clothes that may have come into contact with wine or the fat of a pig during their tailoring. Should we pray in them before washing them?"
They replied: "Yes, there is no harm. Allah (swt) has only forbidden eating and drinking it (pork and wine) but has not forbidden wearing it, touching it, or praying in it."
